description_long: This object is a large, multi-barreled rotary machine gun, commonly known as a minigun or Gatling-style weapon. It features six parallel cylindrical barrels arranged in a circular cluster, each supported by a robust frame and a visible motor housing beneath the barrel assembly. The rear section includes a prominent pistol-style grip and a secondary support handle, with visible wiring and mechanical components suggesting an electrically driven firing mechanism. The main body is constructed from dark, matte-finished metal, with some exposed mechanical parts and a compact, boxy ammunition feed system attached to the underside. The overall design is utilitarian, emphasizing durability and rapid-fire capability, with minimal decorative elements. This type of weapon is typically used in military or law enforcement scenarios, but could also appear in urban environments as a mounted or portable heavy weapon.
description: A heavy, six-barreled rotary machine gun with a matte black metal finish, featuring a pistol grip, support handle, and visible motor housing. The design is robust and utilitarian, built for rapid-fire use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the six-barrel cluster, motor housing, and both grip handles.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular arrangement of the six barrels and the compact, symmetrical frame.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the barrel assembly and support structure.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the pistol grip, support handle, and the alignment of the barrels.
attribute_barrel_type: multi-barrel rotary cluster with smooth cylindrical barrels
